$comment: What the agent receives in CLASSIC mode (mcp-server-classic.ts) for the same operation as 01-clean-success.json
$mode_difference: In composable mode the agent calls the `bash` tool with a shell command. In classic mode it calls each registry skill as a separate MCP tool with its own JSONSchema-typed args. Same execution path underneath; different surface to the model.
$tradeoff: Composable: 1 tool definition in tools/list (~200 tokens), longer command output (the model writes bash). Classic: N tool definitions in tools/list (one per skill, ~200 tokens × N), shorter structured args. With output typically 2-10× input price, the cheaper mode depends on round count and which side dominates.
